mappings.rationale: Covers the engineering principles behind machines, machine components, and machine tools that form the core of this section.
Directly covers robots and computer-aided machining, key topics under the section's computer-aided machining and nonconventional methods.
Relevant to computer numerical control, CAD/CAM, and computer-integrated manufacturing covered in the section.
Covers the materials science underlying tool construction and the properties of metals and other materials shaped by machine tools.
Relevant to nonconventional methods of machining including electron-beam, ion beam, and laser machining at very small scales.
Covers the historical development of machine tools and the transition from hand tools to power-driven machinery.
Relevant to the early history of hand tools including Paleolithic and Neolithic stone tools.
Covers the development of early stone and metal hand tools in the Paleolithic and Neolithic periods.
Relevant to computer-integrated manufacturing and the AI systems that drive modern automated machining.
